inputs.name: certificates
inputs.description: The certificates that are to be encapsulated within the S/MIME entity. Each byte[ ] represents a single certificate.

outputs.name: SMimeEnvStream
outputs.description: S/MIME entity.

inputs.name: envStream
inputs.description: MIME message that you want to encrypt (for example, the output produced by pub.mime:getEnvelopeStream ).

inputs.name: recipientCerts
inputs.description: The X.509 certificates of the recipients for whom this message will be encrypted. Each element in the list represents a certificate for a single recipient in the form of a byte[ ]. Note: When you have multiple recipients, createEncryptedData creates a single message that is encrypted for all recipients. It does not create a separate message for each recipient.

inputs.name: encryptionAlg
inputs.description: Optional. Code specifying the encryption algorithm to use. Must be TripleDES (default), DES , or RC2 . Note: If you have enabled the FIPS (Federal Information Processing Standards) mode , then Integration Server uses only TripleDES .

inputs.name: keyLength
inputs.description: Optional. Length of the encryption key for RC2 encryption. Must be 40 , 64 , or 128 (default). This parameter is ignored if encryptionAlg is not RC2.

outputs.name: SMimeEnvStream
outputs.description: The encrypted MIME message.

inputs.name: envStream
inputs.description: The MIME message that you want to sign and encrypt (for example, the output produced by pub.mime:getEnvelopeStream ).

inputs.name: privKey
inputs.description: Private key of the party signing the message.

inputs.name: certificates
inputs.description: Optional. The certificate chain of the party signing the message, where each byte[ ] represents a single certificate in the chain. Certificates must appear in hierarchical order, starting with the signer's certificate in element 0. The following list shows how the elements of a complete chain would appear for a certificate that was issued through two intermediate CAs:

inputs.name: signerCert
inputs.description: Digital certificate of the party signing the message.

inputs.name: explicit
inputs.description: Optional. Flag indicating whether an implicit or explicit signature is to be generated. Set to: true to generate an explicit (detached) signature. This is the default. false to generate an implicit signature.

inputs.name: recipientCerts
inputs.description: X.509 certificates of the recipients for whom this message will be encrypted. Each element in the list contains the certificate for a single recipient in the form of a byte array.

inputs.name: encryptionAlg
inputs.description: Optional. Code specifying the encryption algorithm to use. Must be TripleDES (default), DES , or RC2 . Note: If you have enabled the FIPS (Federal Information Processing Standards) mode , then Integration Server uses only TripleDES .

inputs.name: keyLength
inputs.description: Optional. Length of the encryption key for RC2 encryption. Must be 40 , 64 , or 128 (default). This parameter is ignored if encryptionAlg is not RC2.

outputs.name: SMimeEnvStream
outputs.description: Signed and encrypted MIME message.

inputs.name: envStream
inputs.description: MIME message that you want to sign (for example, the output produced by pub.mime:getEnvelopeStream ).

inputs.name: privKey
inputs.description: Private key of the party signing the message.

inputs.name: certificates
inputs.description: Optional. Certificate chain of the party that signed the message, where each byte[ ] represents a single certificate in the chain. Certificates must appear in hierarchical order, starting with the signer's certificate in element 0. The following shows how the elements of a complete chain would appear for a certificate that was issued through two intermediate CAs:

inputs.name: signerCert
inputs.description: Digital certificate of the party signing the message.

inputs.name: explicit
inputs.description: Optional. Flag indicating whether an implicit or explicit signature is generated. Set to: true to generate an explicit (detached) signature. This is the default. false to generate an implicit signature.

outputs.name: SMimeEnvStream
outputs.description: The signed MIME message.

inputs.name: SMimeEnvStream
inputs.description: The certs-only S/MIME entity.

outputs.name: certificates
outputs.description: The extracted certificates. Each element in the list contains one of the extracted certificates represented as a byte[ ].

inputs.name: SMimeEnvStream
inputs.description: The encrypted S/MIME entity (for example, the output produced by pub.smime:createEncryptedData ).

inputs.name: recipientCert
inputs.description: Digital certificate of the party receiving the message.

inputs.name: privKey
inputs.description: Private key of the party receiving the message (that is, the party whose public key was used to encrypt the message).

outputs.name: mimeData
outputs.description: MIME object containing the decrypted MIME message.

outputs.name: contentDigest
outputs.description: Message digest of the encrypted content, base64-encoded. (Some sites return this digest to the sender to acknowledge their receipt of the message.)

outputs.name: encrypted
outputs.description: Conditional. Flag indicating whether the decrypted MIME entity is encrypted. A value of: true indicates that the MIME entity is encrypted. false indicates that the MIME entity is not encrypted.

outputs.name: signed
outputs.description: Conditional. Flag indicating whether the decrypted MIME entity is signed. A value of: true indicates that the MIME entity is signed. false indicates that the MIME entity is not signed.

outputs.name: certsOnly
outputs.description: Conditional. Flag indicating whether the decrypted MIME entity is a certs-only entity. A value of: true indicates that the MIME entity is a certs-only entity. false indicates that the MIME entity is not a certs-only entity.

outputs.name: stream
outputs.description: Conditional. The decrypted MIME entity.

inputs.name: SMimeEnvStream
inputs.description: Signed MIME entity (for example, the output produced by pub.smime:createSignedData ).

inputs.name: signerCertChain
inputs.description: Optional. Certificate chain of the party that signed the message, where each byte[ ] represents a single certificate in the chain. Certificates must appear in hierarchical order, starting with the signer's certificate in element 0. The following shows how the elements of a complete chain would appear for a certificate that was issued through two intermediate CAs:

outputs.name: mimeData
outputs.description: MIME object containing the extracted MIME entity.

outputs.name: contentDigest
outputs.description: Message digest (base64-encoded) that was recalculated by processSignedData .

outputs.name: signerCert
outputs.description: Signer's X.509 certificate.

outputs.name: encrypted
outputs.description: Conditional. Flag indicating whether the extracted MIME entity is encrypted. A value of: true indicates that the MIME entity is encrypted. false indicates that the MIME entity is not encrypted.

outputs.name: signed
outputs.description: Conditional. Flag indicating whether the extracted MIME entity is signed. A value of: true indicates that the MIME entity is signed. false indicates that the MIME entity is not signed.

outputs.name: certsOnly
outputs.description: Conditional. Flag indicating whether the extracted MIME entity is a certs-only entity. A value of: true indicates that the MIME entity is a certs-only entity. false indicates that the MIME entity is not a certs-only entity.

outputs.name: stream
outputs.description: Conditional. Extracted MIME entity.

outputs.name: verify
outputs.description: Flag indicating whether the signature was successfully processed. Success indicates that the signature was successfully verified with the supplied public key. A value of: true indicates that signature processing was successful. false indicates that signature processing failed. The signature could not be verified because an errorCode 1, 2, 3, or 4 occurred.

outputs.name: trusted
outputs.description: Flag indicating whether the signer certificate is trusted or not. A value of: true indicates that the signer certificate is trusted. false indicates that the signer certificate is not trusted.

outputs.name: errorCode
outputs.description: Conditional. Number indicating the kind of error that occurred while processing the signature. See errorMessage for possible values. If no error occurred, errorCode will not be returned.

outputs.name: errorMessage
outputs.description: Conditional. Textual error message indicating what kind of error occurred while processing the signature. Error codes and messages are as follows:

inputs.name: envStream
inputs.description: The MIME message that you want to sign and encrypt (for example, the output produced by pub.mime:getEnvelopeStream ).

inputs.name: keyStoreAlias
inputs.description: Alias of the keystore containing the signing key.

inputs.name: keyAlias
inputs.description: Alias of the private key to be used for signing.

inputs.name: explicit
inputs.description: Optional. Flag indicating whether an implicit or explicit signature is to be generated. Set to: True to generate an explicit (detached) signature. This is the default. False to generate an implicit signature.

inputs.name: recipientCerts
inputs.description: A list of byte[ ] for the partner certificates for whom this message will be encrypted.

inputs.name: encryptionAlg
inputs.description: Optional. Code specifying the encryption algorithm to use. Must be TripleDES (default), DES, or RC2.

inputs.name: keyLength
inputs.description: Optional. Length of the encryption key for RC2 encryption. Must be 40, 64, or 128 (default). This parameter is ignored if encryptionAlg is not RC2.

inputs.name: signingAlg
inputs.description: Optional. The signing algorithm to use to sign the S/MIME messages. Set to: SHA1 SHA224 SHA256 SHA384 SHA512 MD5 The default is SHA1 . Note: If you do not set this parameter, Integration Server uses SHA512 algorithm to sign the incoming messages. Note: The MD5 signing algorithm is not available if FIPS mode is enabled on Integration Server .

outputs.name: SMimeEnvStream
outputs.description: Signed and encrypted data as a stream.

inputs.name: envStream
inputs.description: MIME message that you want to sign (for example, the output produced by pub.mime:getEnvelopeStream ).

inputs.name: keyStoreAlias
inputs.description: Alias of the keystore.

inputs.name: keyAlias
inputs.description: Alias of the private key of interest in the keystore.

inputs.name: explicit
inputs.description: Optional. Flag indicating whether an implicit or explicit signature is generated. Set to: True to generate an explicit (detached) signature. This is the default. False to generate an implicit signature.

inputs.name: signingAlg
inputs.description: Optional. The signing algorithm to use to sign the S/MIME messages. Set to: SHA1 SHA224 SHA256 SHA384 SHA512 MD5 The default is SHA1 . Note: If you do not set this parameter, Integration Server uses SHA1 algorithm to sign the incoming messages. Note: The MD5 signing algorithm is not available if FIPS mode is enabled on the Integration Server .

outputs.name: SMimeEnvStream
outputs.description: The signed MIME stream.

inputs.name: SMimeEnvStream
inputs.description: The encrypted S/MIME stream.

inputs.name: keyStoreAlias
inputs.description: Alias of the keystore containing the decryption key.

inputs.name: keyAlias
inputs.description: Alias of the key used for decryption.

outputs.name: mimeData
outputs.description: The decrypted MIME message.

outputs.name: contentDigest
outputs.description: Message digest of the encrypted content, base64-encoded. (Some sites return this digest to the sender to acknowledge their receipt of the message.

outputs.name: encrypted
outputs.description: Flag indicating whether the MIME entity passed in to the service was encrypted. A value of: True indicates that the MIME entity was encrypted. False indicates that the MIME entity was not encrypted.

outputs.name: signed
outputs.description: Flag indicating whether the MIME entity passed in to the service was signed. A value of: True indicates that the MIME entity is signed. False indicates that the MIME entity is not signed.

outputs.name: certsOnly
outputs.description: Flag indicating whether the MIME entity passed in to the service contained only digital certificates. A value of: True indicates that the MIME entity is a certs-only entity. False indicates that the MIME entity is not a certs-only entity.

outputs.name: stream
outputs.description: The decrypted MIME entity.
